title: thymus acinos var. vulgaris
intro: Thymus acinos var. vulgaris, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, perennial herb native to Europe and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which help to combat infections and inflammation. The medicinal properties of creeping thyme make it beneficial for respiratory health, with applications in treating coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues. Creeping thyme can be prepared through herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
